prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. An internal conflict in the Central African Republic (CAR) started essentially on 13 April 2013, when the government of President Michel Djotodia officially took over. The fighting was between the government of the Central African Republic's former Séléka coalition of rebel groups, who are mainly from the Muslim minority, and the mainly Christian anti-balaka coalition. The conflict was part of the ongoing Central African Republic Civil War (2012–present). International organisations, such as the United Nations, had warned of a possible genocide. UNSC resolution 2122 authorised the African-led International Support Mission to the Central African Republic (MISCA) to be deployed to the country, and France to lead operations with additional troops sent to bolster its force in the country. Following a summit of Economic Community of Central African States
B. On 13 April 2013, the government of President Michel Djotodia took control of the Central African Republic (CAR), leading to an internal conflict. The conflict involved the government of the former Séléka coalition, a coalition of rebel groups from the Muslim minority and the Christian anti-balaka coalition. This conflict was part of the ongoing Central African Republic Civil War (2012-present) and involved international organizations, including the United Nations, who had warned of a potential genocide. UNSC resolution 2122 authorized the African-led International Support Mission and France to lead additional troops
C. A domestic dispute within the Central African Republic (CAR) principally commenced on April 13, 2013, coinciding with the formal assumption of power by President Michel Djotodia's administration. This confrontation pitted the government of the Central African Republic's erstwhile Séléka alliance of insurgent factions, predominantly composed of the Muslim demographic, against the largely Christian anti-balaka alliance. This particular dispute was a component of the protracted Central African Republic Civil War, which began in 2012 and continues to the present day. Global bodies, including the United Nations, had issued cautionary statements regarding the potential for widespread ethnic cleansing. United Nations Security Council Resolution 2122 granted permission for the deployment of the African-led International Support Mission to the Central African Republic (MISCA) to the nation, and for France to spearhead military actions with supplementary forces dispatched to augment its presence in the country. Subsequent to a gathering of the Economic Community of Central African States.

Answer:

options.A: An internal conflict in the Central African Republic (CAR) started essentially on 13 April 2013, when the government of President Michel Djotodia officially took over. The fighting was between the government of the Central African Republic's former Séléka coalition of rebel groups, who are mainly from the Muslim minority, and the mainly Christian anti-balaka coalition. The conflict was part of the ongoing Central African Republic Civil War (2012–present). International organisations, such as the United Nations, had warned of a possible genocide. UNSC resolution 2122 authorised the African-led International Support Mission to the Central African Republic (MISCA) to be deployed to the country, and France to lead operations with additional troops sent to bolster its force in the country. Following a summit of Economic Community of Central African States
options.B: On 13 April 2013, the government of President Michel Djotodia took control of the Central African Republic (CAR), leading to an internal conflict. The conflict involved the government of the former Séléka coalition, a coalition of rebel groups from the Muslim minority and the Christian anti-balaka coalition. This conflict was part of the ongoing Central African Republic Civil War (2012-present) and involved international organizations, including the United Nations, who had warned of a potential genocide. UNSC resolution 2122 authorized the African-led International Support Mission and France to lead additional troops
options.C: A domestic dispute within the Central African Republic (CAR) principally commenced on April 13, 2013, coinciding with the formal assumption of power by President Michel Djotodia's administration. This confrontation pitted the government of the Central African Republic's erstwhile Séléka alliance of insurgent factions, predominantly composed of the Muslim demographic, against the largely Christian anti-balaka alliance. This particular dispute was a component of the protracted Central African Republic Civil War, which began in 2012 and continues to the present day. Global bodies, including the United Nations, had issued cautionary statements regarding the potential for widespread ethnic cleansing. United Nations Security Council Resolution 2122 granted permission for the deployment of the African-led International Support Mission to the Central African Republic (MISCA) to the nation, and for France to spearhead military actions with supplementary forces dispatched to augment its presence in the country. Subsequent to a gathering of the Economic Community of Central African States.
